title: Pecteilis baeuerlenii
taxonomy: Pecteilis baeuerlenii is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Pecteilis, which is a group of orchids commonly known as lady orchids. Pecteilis baeuerlenii is further classified as a member of the tribe Cranichideae, a subfamily of orchids characterized by their often subterranean habit. The species is also known by its synonym, Pecteilis nephelophora. Its classification reflects the plant's unique growth habits and characteristics within the larger orchid family.

intro: Pecteilis baeuerlenii, also known as the "Lady's Slipper Orchid," is a type of terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pecteilis baeuerlenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Pecteilis baeuerlenii is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of ailments.
